name: Contact address for consignment (branch address entry)
pageTitle: Contact address for consignment - Import and export applications - GOV.UK
caption: Contacts
heading: Contact address for consignment
bodyCopy: This is the contact address of the person responsible for the consignment from when it enters Great Britain until authorities complete their checks.
If the correct address is not shown, add a new branch address, then return to this page.

structure.text: This is the contact address of the person responsible for the consignment from when it enters Great Britain until authorities complete their checks.
If the correct address is not shown, add a new branch address, then return to this page.
structure.govukComponent: Hint (govuk-body govuk-hint) containing an inline link

structure.text: Address-book branch addresses, one radio per saved address (first is pre-checked)

govukComponents.usage: Return to previous page (href="#", history back)
Groups the address-selection radios under the legend 'Select an address'
One radio per saved address-book branch address; user picks the consignment contact address

'Save and return to hub' and 'Save and continue' primary submit buttons

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: class="branch-address-select" (custom, non-govuk hook class on the radios form-group/fieldset)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: IPAFFS-specific styling/JS hook class attached to the govuk-form-group container AND to every govuk-radios__item in the address selection block (verified: 1 DIV.govuk-form-group.branch-address-select + 16 DIV.govuk-radios__item.branch-address-select = 17 elements carrying the class). Note the class name collides with the radio inputs' name attribute (name=branch-address-select), which are two different things.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Purely a bespoke hook class; carries no semantic or layout meaning that govuk-frontend does not already provide. Not a real custom widget — the control is a standard govuk Radios.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: None needed — keep the standard Radios component; the hook class can be dropped in the rebuild.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 87 class inventory: querySelectorAll('.branch-address-select') = 17 elements = 1 govuk-form-group + 16 govuk-radios__item

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: govuk-body govuk-hint applied to the lead/intro paragraph (not associated with a form control via aria-describedby)
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: Renders the introductory explanatory paragraph in the muted grey 'hint' style rather than standard body text.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Minor deviation — govuk-hint is intended for form-control hint text, not standalone lead copy. Cosmetic only.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Use a plain govuk-body paragraph (or govuk-body-l lead) for the intro; reserve govuk-hint for actual field hints.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 87 structure eval (P.govuk-body govuk-hint)

fields.name: branch-address-select
fields.label: Select an address
fields.hint: If the correct address is not shown, add a new branch address, then return to this page.
fields.requiredEvidence: Legacy makes the address selection mandatory for CHED-D on two independent layers. (1) Frontend Joi: BRANCH_ADDRESS_KEY 'branch-address-select' is Joi.string().required().label('Organisation Branch Address') at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/organisation_branch_address_validation.js:25 (label/key at utils/validation_constants.js:12-13); the handler runs this on every POST at routes/handlers/importer/organisation_branch_address.js:32-36 and re-renders the page with the inline error. (2) Backend schema: personResponsible.address (List<String>) is @NotEmpty for the NotificationSingleCedValidation group (single-CED = CHED-D single journey) at ipaffs-imports-notification-schema/.../representation/Party.java:54-64. So required=true, superseding the trace-only 'required=false' (no error state was ever rendered because the happy path always selects/creates an address). NOTE: legacy mandatoriness is 'as the old system had it' — the rebuild may revisit whether a pre-checked default '0' address makes an explicit selection redundant.
options: 34 GREYSTONE PARK, CREWE, CHESHIRE EAST, CW1 2AL / United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland
43 West Hague Extension / Main street / Castlereagh / Belfast / 78145 / Switzerland
43 East Hague Extension / Delectus sit odio p / Laborum Odio tempor / Quas occaecat ut ear / 30055
fields.conditionalOn: Always shown. Options are the current user's saved address-book branch addresses (reference/user data), not a fixed list — the rebuild sources these from the address book, not hardcoded. First option value is '0' (the default/primary address-book address, pre-checked); the remainder carry the address record UUID as their value. The 16 radios observed were the seeded address-book fixtures (with duplicate '43 West/East Hague Extension' entries), not distinct real addresses. NOTE: The QA workflow never selects an existing radio directly — the happy-path ched-d journey always clicks 'add a new branch address', creates a new one, and returns (workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:342-353). So the ContactAddressPage page object exposes only linkAddNewBranchAddress + btnSaveAndContinue (page-objects/notification/ContactAddressPage.ts) and never asserts against the radio list — the radios are trace-only evidence.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 87 control inventory: 16 radios (name=branch-address-select), first value=0 [checked], remaining 15 are address-record UUIDs. Independently corroborated as 16 in trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 action 91 (querySelectorAll('input[name=branch-address-select]').length = 16). NB: 17 elements carry the CSS hook class .branch-address-select (1 govuk-form-group container + 16 govuk-radios__item) — that 17 is almost certainly the source of the earlier '17 radios' miscount; the actual radio-input count is 16.

fields.name: company-name
fields.label: Branch address name
fields.requiredEvidence: Joi.string().max(255).empty('').required() at ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/economic_operator.js:26-29 (the Add-branch-address / economic-operator-create form). Confirms the GDS-convention inference (no '(optional)' suffix). Legacy required-error copy: 'Enter a branch name'; max-length copy: 'Branch name must be 255 characters or fewer'. NB discrepancy: the on-page visible label is 'Branch address name' (rendered/page-object) but the error message uses the label 'Branch name' (labels/en.js:73 ORGANISATION_BRANCH_ADDRESS_NAME).
fields.conditionalOn: Shown on the 'Add branch address' sub-page reached by clicking 'add a new branch address' (URL .../traders/organisation branch address/new). Page title: 'Add branch address'. H1: 'Add branch address'. HTML input name is 'company-name'. Page-object label confirms the visible field label is 'Branch address name'. GDS convention: label has no '(optional)' suffix, so likely a mandatory field (no validation trace to confirm — see openQuestions).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 88 (a11y snapshot textbox 'Branch address name' + control inventory name=company-name); page object BranchAddressCreationPage.inputBranchAddressName (getByRole textbox 'Branch address name') at page-objects/notification/BranchAddressCreationPage.ts:8-10; domain type Address.name (required string) at types/address.ts

fields.name: address-line-1
fields.label: Address line 1
fields.requiredEvidence: Joi.string().max(255).empty('').required() at economic_operator.js:30-33. Confirms the GDS-convention inference. Legacy required copy: 'Enter an address line 1'; max copy: 'Address line 1 must be 255 characters or fewer' (labels/en.js:4).
fields.conditionalOn: Add branch address sub-page; inside the 'Branch address' fieldset. GDS convention: label has no '(optional)' suffix, so likely mandatory (no validation trace to confirm).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 88 (a11y snapshot + control inventory name=address-line-1); page object BranchAddressCreationPage.inputAddressLine1 (getByRole textbox 'Address line 1') at page-objects/notification/BranchAddressCreationPage.ts:12-14; domain type Address.addressLine1 at types/address.ts

fields.label: Address line 2 (optional)

fields.conditionalOn: Add branch address sub-page; inside the 'Branch address' fieldset. Label carries the '(optional)' suffix — genuinely optional per GDS convention. (The QA fillAddress still populates it, but that is test data, not a requirement.)
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 88 (a11y snapshot + control inventory name=address-line-2); page object BranchAddressCreationPage.inputAddressLine2 (getByRole textbox 'Address line 2 (optional)') at page-objects/notification/BranchAddressCreationPage.ts:16-18; domain type Address.addressLine2 at types/address.ts

fields.label: Address line 3 (optional)

fields.conditionalOn: Add branch address sub-page; inside the 'Branch address' fieldset. Label carries the '(optional)' suffix — genuinely optional per GDS convention.
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 88 (a11y snapshot + control inventory name=address-line-3); page object BranchAddressCreationPage.inputAddressLine3 (getByRole textbox 'Address line 3 (optional)') at page-objects/notification/BranchAddressCreationPage.ts:20-22; domain type Address.addressLine3 at types/address.ts

fields.name: city-or-town
fields.label: City or town
fields.requiredEvidence: Joi.string().max(58).empty('').required() at economic_operator.js:40-43. Confirms the GDS-convention inference. Legacy required copy: 'Enter a city or town'; max copy (58, not 255): 'City or town must be 58 characters or fewer' (labels/en.js:23).
fields.conditionalOn: Add branch address sub-page; inside the 'Branch address' fieldset. GDS convention: no '(optional)' suffix, so likely mandatory (no validation trace to confirm).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 88 (a11y snapshot + control inventory name=city-or-town); page object BranchAddressCreationPage.inputCityOrTown (getByRole textbox 'City or town') at page-objects/notification/BranchAddressCreationPage.ts:24-26; domain type Address.cityOrTown at types/address.ts

fields.name: postcode
fields.label: Postcode or ZIP code
fields.requiredEvidence: Joi.string().max(32).allow('') at economic_operator.js:49-51 — NO .required(), so postcode is OPTIONAL. This CONTRADICTS the earlier GDS-convention inference (the visible label 'Postcode or ZIP code' carries no '(optional)' suffix, which usually signals mandatory). Legacy wins on the rule: required=false. Only a max-length rule applies: 'Post code must be 32 characters or fewer' (note the error label is 'Post code', two words, from labels/en.js:81 — differs from the on-page label 'Postcode or ZIP code'). See discrepancies.
fields.conditionalOn: Add branch address sub-page; inside the 'Branch address' fieldset. GDS convention: no '(optional)' suffix, so likely mandatory (no validation trace to confirm).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 88 (a11y snapshot + control inventory name=postcode); page object BranchAddressCreationPage.inputPostcodeOrZipCode (getByRole textbox 'Postcode or ZIP code') at page-objects/notification/BranchAddressCreationPage.ts:28-30; domain type Address.postcode at types/address.ts

fields.name: telephone
fields.label: Telephone number
fields.requiredEvidence: Joi.string().max(30).empty('').required() at economic_operator.js:52-55. Confirms the GDS-convention inference. Legacy required copy: 'Enter a telephone'; max copy: 'Telephone must be 30 characters or fewer' (error label 'Telephone' at labels/en.js:97 differs from the on-page label 'Telephone number').
fields.conditionalOn: Add branch address sub-page; inside the 'Branch address' fieldset. HTML input type=tel. GDS convention: no '(optional)' suffix, so likely mandatory (no validation trace to confirm).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 88 (a11y snapshot + control inventory name=telephone type=tel); page object BranchAddressCreationPage.inputTelephoneNumber (getByRole textbox 'Telephone number') at page-objects/notification/BranchAddressCreationPage.ts:32-34; domain type Address.telephone at types/address.ts

fields.name: country
fields.label: Country
fields.requiredEvidence: Joi.string().valid(...countries).empty('').required() at economic_operator.js:56-59. Required. Legacy required copy: 'Enter a country'; invalid-value copy: 'Country is not valid' (labels/en.js:34). Template renders it as a govuk/select with topOption 'Please select your country' and items=countriesSelectItems (economic_operator_create.html:141-151, isOrganisationBranchAddress branch) — a plain select, matching the trace (not an autocomplete).
fields.conditionalOn: Add branch address sub-page; inside the 'Branch address' fieldset. Plain govuk-select (NOT an accessible-autocomplete). 254 options incl. the '' / 'Please select your country' placeholder; option values are ISO 3166 alpha-2 codes (AF, AX, AL...). Reference data — the rebuild should source the country list from a reference-data service, not hardcode it. The QA journey selects the value 'GB-ENG' (country.England, a GB-subdivision code, NOT a plain alpha-2 country code) — the select therefore also carries GB-subdivision entries (GB-ENG/GB-SCT/GB-WLS/GB-NIR) alongside the alpha-2 list; this is a value-space discrepancy worth flagging (see openQuestions).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 88 (control inventory SELECT name=country; option-count eval = 254, first values ''=Please select your country, AF=Afghanistan, AX=Aland Islands, AL=Albania). Observed value 'GB-ENG' is inferred from the driving test data: DEFAULT_CONFIG.branchAddress.country = country.England = 'GB-ENG' at workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:139 and types/country.ts:238; selected via BranchAddressCreationPage.dropdownCountry (getByLabel 'Country').selectOption at page-objects/notification/BranchAddressCreationPage.ts:44-46,58

fields.name: email
fields.label: Email address
fields.requiredEvidence: Joi.string().lowercase().email(...).max(255).empty('').required() at economic_operator.js:44-48. Required AND format-validated. Legacy required copy: 'Enter an email address'; format copy: 'Enter an email address in the correct format, like name@example.com' (messages/en.js:72); max copy: 'Email address must be 255 characters or fewer' (labels/en.js:46).
fields.conditionalOn: Add branch address sub-page; inside the 'Branch address' fieldset. HTML input type=email. GDS convention: no '(optional)' suffix, so likely mandatory (no validation trace to confirm).
fields.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f action 88 (a11y snapshot + control inventory name=email type=email); page object BranchAddressCreationPage.inputEmailAddress (getByRole textbox 'Email address') at page-objects/notification/BranchAddressCreationPage.ts:40-42; domain type Address.email at types/address.ts

validationMessages.message: No validation error state was RENDERED in either evidence trace — both are error-free happy-path submissions, and the QA repo (tests/notification/ched-d/*.spec.ts, tests/accessibility/ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts) asserts no error copy. The messages below were therefore recovered from the authorised legacy IPAFFS source, not observed rendering. They remain 'legacy', not 'confirmed'.
validationMessages.trigger: Submitting the selection page with no address chosen, or saving the Add-branch-address form with missing/invalid fields.
validationMessages.evidence: trace eddc6f6ec852d692c454d319ade1607727a84a4f (0 errors) and trace b3742f19b6eea5d9285f5c8739571523a0a13ec1 (0 errors); no .govuk-error-summary present. No error assertion in tests/notification/ched-d/*.spec.ts or tests/accessibility/ched-d-accessibility-tests.spec.ts.

validationMessages.trigger: Selection page ('Contact address for consignment'): 'Save and continue' / 'Save and return to hub' pressed with no address radio selected. This is the inline front-end (Joi) error rendered back on this page. NB the string is Joi's default '.required()' template applied to the field's .label(), so the exact wording is framework-generated, not hand-authored copy.
validationMessages.evidence: ipaffs-frontend-notification/service/src/validation/routes/handlers/importer/organisation_branch_address_validation.js:25 (Joi.string().required().label(BRANCH_ADDRESS_LABEL)); label 'Organisation Branch Address' + key 'branch-address-select' at utils/validation_constants.js:12-13; rendered via handler POST at routes/handlers/importer/organisation_branch_address.js:32-36 + errorSpan in views/importer/organisationBranchAddress.html:40.

openQuestions: RESOLVED from legacy (2026-07-18): Validation copy and mandatoriness are now recovered from the authorised IPAFFS source (still 'legacy', not rendered). Selection radio 'branch-address-select' is REQUIRED for CHED-D (front-end Joi + backend SingleCed @NotEmpty) — inline error '"Organisation Branch Address" is required', review/submit error 'Add the contact address for consignment'. Add-branch-address form: company-name, address-line-1, city-or-town, telephone, email and country are all REQUIRED; address-line-2, address-line-3 and — contrary to the earlier GDS-convention inference — postcode are OPTIONAL. Full per-field required/format/max-length copy is in validationMessages. Remaining true gap: none of these were ever RENDERED, so an errored trace would still be needed to promote them to 'confirmed'.

The 'Save and return to hub' vs 'Save and continue' distinction on the selection page: both are govuk-button primary style (no --secondary modifier observed). Confirm intended routing difference (hub vs next journey page) against legacy source. The QA page object only wires btnSaveAndContinue (page-objects/notification/ContactAddressPage.ts:9), so 'Save and return to hub' routing is un-exercised by tests.

The address-selection radio list is address-book/user data, not a fixed reference list — the 16 radios seen were seeded test fixtures (with duplicates). The rebuild must source these from the address book. Confirm the exact address-record shape (single-line UK address vs multi-line international) rendered per radio. Note: the happy-path QA journey never picks an existing radio (it always adds a new branch address then returns), so the direct-selection path is entirely trace-only and un-asserted.

The Country <select> on the Add branch address form is a plain govuk-select here (254 options). Trace option values are ISO alpha-2 (AF, AX, AL...), but the QA journey selects 'GB-ENG' (a GB-subdivision code, not alpha-2) — so the select mixes country codes with GB-subdivision codes. Confirm the intended value-space for the rebuild (alpha-2 + GB subdivisions, or ISO 3166-2?) and whether this field should be an accessible-autocomplete rather than a plain select.

RESOLVED (partially): The 'Add branch address' flow is a distinct sub-flow, not just a modal. The QA repo models it as three separate page objects — BranchAddressCreationPage (title/H1 'Add branch address', its own 'Save and continue'), then a BranchAddressConfirmationPage carrying a single 'Return to notification' button, then a return to the creation/selection page (workflows/notification/ched-d-workflows.ts:345-353; page-objects/notification/BranchAddressConfirmationPage.ts). So creating a branch address is a three-page interstitial sub-journey off contact-address: enter address -> confirmation with 'Return to notification' -> back to the selection page with the new address now available to pick. Remaining question: capture the confirmation page's copy/summary (title, H1, what it echoes back) from a trace — it is currently only evidenced via the page object's single button, not a rendered snapshot in this spec.
